subject: Databases
title: Read Committed
description: A fast breakdown of Read Committed for builders.

hook.text: Transaction B only sees data that A has committed. No dirty reads. This is Read Committed.

buildup.text: Each statement in B sees the latest committed state at that moment. Two SELECTs may see different data.

discovery.text: This is the default in Postgres and Oracle. It's good enough for most applications.

twist.text: Non-repeatable reads are possible: read a value, another transaction changes it, read again — different result.

climax.text: For CRUD apps, Read Committed works. For financial calculations, you may need stronger isolation.

punchline.text: Read Committed: no dirty reads, but values can change between reads.

quiz.question: What anomaly is possible under Read Committed isolation?
options: Non-repeatable reads — reading the same row twice yields different values
No anomalies are possible under Read Committed
